tool.name: COCO Annotation Visualizer
tool.description: Draw COCO bounding boxes, segmentation masks, and labels.
tool.command: mkdir -p data_files && #for $indata in $source ln -s '$indata' 'data_files/${indata.element_identifier}.${indata.ext}' && #end for python '$__tool_directory__/coco_annotation_visualizer.py' --annotations '$coco_annotations' --outdir outputs #if $filter_categories --filter_categories '$filter_categories' #end if --show_bbox '$display.show_bbox' --show_mask '$display.show_mask' --show_labels '$display.show_labels' --show_count '$display.show_count' --mask_opacity '$display.mask_opacity' --bbox_thickness '$display.bbox_thickness' --color_mode '$display.color_mode' --font_scale '$display.font_scale' --output_format '$display.output_format' --output_mode '$output_mode.mode' #if $output_mode.mode in ['video', 'both'] --video_fps '$output_mode.video_fps' #end if

param.name: source
param.label: Input images
param.help: Select one or more images or a list collection. Filenames must match those in the COCO JSON.

validator.message: TIFF images must contain exactly 3 channels (RGB).

param.name: filter_categories
param.label: Filter categories (optional)
param.help: Comma-separated list of category names to display. Leave empty to show all categories.

param.name: show_count
param.label: Show annotation count
param.help: Draws the total number of visible annotations in the top-left corner of each image.

param.name: mask_opacity
param.label: Mask opacity
param.help: Transparency of segmentation mask overlays (0 = transparent, 1 = opaque).

param.name: font_scale
param.label: Label font scale
param.help: Controls the size of category name text on the image.

param.name: color_mode
param.label: Color mode
param.option: Per category (same color for each class)
Per instance (different color for each annotation)

param.name: mode
param.label: Output mode
param.help: Choose whether to output annotated frames, a compiled video, or both.
param.option: Video (compile all frames into a single video)

param.name: video_fps
param.label: Video frame rate (FPS)
param.help:
Controls the speed of the output video. The total number of frames always equals the number of annotated images, only the playback speed changes.
Example: 60 annotated images at 60 FPS gives a 1 second video, at 10 FPS the same frames give a 6 second video.

param.name: video_fps
param.label: Video frame rate (FPS)
param.help:
Controls the speed of the output video. The total number of frames always equals the number of annotated images, only the playback speed changes.
Example: 60 annotated images at 60 FPS gives a 1 second video, at 10 FPS the same frames give a 6 second video.

tool.help:
================================= COCO Annotation Visualizer =================================
Overlay COCO annotations (bounding boxes, segmentation masks, category labels) onto your images.
------------------- Inputs -------------------
- **Images**: one or more .jpg / .png / .tiff files, or a Galaxy list collection. - **COCO annotation file**: a JSON file strictly following the COCO format. It must contain three top-level keys:
- ``images``: list of image entries, each with at least ``id``, ``file_name``, ``width``, and ``height``. - ``categories``: list of class definitions, each with ``id`` and ``name``. - ``annotations``: list of annotation entries, each with ``id``, ``image_id``, ``category_id``, and at least one of ``bbox`` (``[x, y, width, height]``) or ``segmentation`` (polygon list or RLE).
The tool matches annotation entries to input images using the ``file_name`` field (case-insensitive). Images present in the JSON but absent from the input dataset are silently skipped; images present in the input but absent from the JSON are passed through without annotations.
.. note::
Image filenames are matched to COCO entries by filename (case-insensitive). If your Galaxy dataset name differs from the original filename, rename it using the pencil icon → *Name* before running the tool.
------------------- Filtering -------------------
Use **Filter categories** to display only a subset of classes, for example::
elephant, bird, fish
Leave the field empty to display all categories present in the annotation file.
------------------- Display options -------------------
- **Bounding boxes**: colored rectangles around each detected object. - **Segmentation masks**: semi-transparent color fills drawn from polygon or RLE data. - **Labels**: category name drawn above the bounding box. - **Annotation count**: total number of visible annotations shown in the top-left corner. - **Color mode**: *per category* assigns a fixed color to each class; *per instance* gives each annotation its own color.
------------------- Output mode -------------------
Choose how the annotated frames are returned:
- **Frames** *(default)*: a Galaxy list collection of annotated images, one per input image (PNG or JPEG). - **Video**: a single MP4 video compiled from all annotated frames in filename order. - **Both**: the frame collection *and* the video.
When compiling a video, set **Video frame rate (FPS)** to match the original acquisition rate (e.g. 25 fps for PAL video, 30 fps for NTSC).
